见.origin: An eye 目 on top of a person's legs 儿 — someone standing up to see and meet the world.

老.origin: A person bent over with long hair leaning on a cane — old and wise.

sentence.en: The old grandpa is walking in the park.

师.origin: Think of a skilled master who has gathered lots of knowledge to share — a teacher.

们.origin: A person 亻 standing by a door 门 — and many people make a group, so 们 means 'they/we'.

方.origin: Picture a flat, square shape with straight sides — 方 means square and direction.

向.origin: A little window 口 in a wall shows which way to face — 向 means toward a direction.

sentence.en: I say hello to the teacher.

面.origin: A drawing of a person's face with the eye inside — 面 means face or surface.

sentence.en: There is a little river ahead.

太.origin: A big person 大 with one extra dot — even bigger! So 太 means too much or very.

sentence.en: The sun is really strong today.

阳.origin: A sunny hillside 阝 lit by the sun 日 — 阳 means sunshine and the bright side.

个.origin: A person 人 with a little tally mark below — 个 is the word we use to count things.

季.origin: Grain 禾 above a child 子 — when seeds grow, it's the season for planting.

知.origin: An arrow 矢 and a mouth 口 — speak as quick and sure as an arrow when you know something.

唱.origin: A mouth 口 next to 昌 (bright and cheery) — open your mouth to sing happily!

叶.origin: A mouth 口 plus ten 十 — picture many little green tongues on a tree: leaves!

sentence.en: In autumn the leaves turn yellow.

新.origin: An axe 斤 chopping fresh wood from a tree — newly cut means new and fresh.

到.origin: Arrive 至 beside a standing knife 刂 — you've reached the spot, you've arrived!

闹.origin: A busy market 市 right inside the gate 门 — crowded and noisy, that's 闹!

穿.origin: A cave 穴 and a tooth 牙 — sharp enough to bore right through, so 穿 means pierce or wear.

sentence.en: Today I am wearing red clothes.

戴.origin: Picture lifting something high to put on your head — 戴 means to wear a hat or glasses.

sentence.en: He is wearing a pair of glasses.

帽.origin: A cloth 巾 placed on top 冒 of your head — a soft little hat to keep you warm.

祝.origin: A person 兄 standing before an altar 礻 with good wishes — 祝 means to wish or bless.

体.origin: A person 亻 next to root 本 — the body is the root that holds the whole person together.

sentence.en: Exercising more keeps the body healthy.
